subject: Geography
title: Will Cities Save the Planet?
description: A fast breakdown of Will Cities Save the Planet? for builders.

hook.text: Cities cover just 3% of Earth's surface but produce over 70% of global carbon emissions.

buildup.text: Dense cities are surprisingly efficient per person—shared transit, smaller homes, shorter trips.

discovery.text: New Yorkers have a lower carbon footprint than the average American, thanks to density.

twist.text: If megacities go green, they could cut global emissions faster than any other single change.

climax.text: The city is both the biggest part of the problem and the biggest part of the solution.

punchline.text: Three percent of the land, seventy percent of the problem—and the solution.

quiz.question: Why do dense city residents often have lower carbon footprints?
options: Shared transit, smaller homes, and shorter trips
